criteria_map: Live autonomous polling disabled: satisfied by live status `polling.enabled=false` and `polling.running=false`.
Docs describe explicit trigger-based staging CI/CD: satisfied by updated architecture/runbook docs and guard assertions.
Repository guards prevent normal-path autonomous polling drift: satisfied by `scripts/ci/test_release_controller_ci.py` assertions on config/docs.
Existing focused tests pass: satisfied by pytest and lint results.
Subsequent push does not auto-start a release run: satisfied by status checks after pushing `b64652a2` and `53a901b5`, with no automatic run for the pushed commits.
evidence: Live `/v1/status` reports `polling.enabled=false`, `polling.running=false`, and `branch_heads={}` after cleanup and diagnostic dry-run.
Live `/v1/polls/once` with `dry_run=true` returns a planned diagnostic result but leaves `branch_heads={}`.
The deployed Release Controller container is healthy on image digest `sha256:71638fd91a55890ad708e47cdfb5bdee96da8a110bed2164210d9e077190a604`.
Local test suites passed: 52 Release Controller tests, 9 parent CI guard tests, and immutable workflow lint.
execution_map: Result `R000` contains implementation, deployment, live verification, and pushed commit evidence.
The work changed runtime config, application code, tests, docs, and live deployment state.
The code path that could re-enable background polling now fails config loading when `polling_enabled=true`.
The API path that could perform branch-head polling now requires `dry_run=true` and cannot execute a real release.
residual_risk: Internal branch rule mode name `auto_deploy` is still semantically awkward but is not an active autonomous trigger path. It is a naming cleanup candidate, not a blocker for the manual-trigger CI/CD objective.
stress_test: Plausible failure mode: a future push to `main` silently triggers staging CI/CD. Tested by pushing commits while live polling was disabled; no automatic release run appeared.
Plausible failure mode: someone calls `/v1/polls/once` without dry-run. Tested live; it returns HTTP 400.
Plausible failure mode: diagnostic dry-run mutates branch-head state and hides later changes. Tested live; branch heads stayed `{}` after dry-run.
C000.summary: The original problem is solved: backend/factory CI/CD no longer starts from autonomous Release Controller branch polling. The live service is disabled for background polling, the new code rejects attempts to re-enable it, diagnostic polling cannot perform real releases, and explicit `/v1/triggers` remains the staging entrypoint.

P000.description: The live Release Controller currently runs autonomous branch polling, so pushes to main can trigger staging CI/CD without an explicit agent/operator release action. For the current agent-driven workflow, CI/CD should be a centralized service flow, but it should start only from explicit Release Controller API requests.
success_criteria: Live Release Controller has autonomous polling disabled and reports polling.enabled=false/running=false.
Repository docs describe explicit trigger-based staging CI/CD as the normal path.
Repository guards fail if sample config or docs reintroduce autonomous polling as the normal backend/factory release path.
Existing Release Controller unit/CI guard tests pass.
A subsequent push to main does not auto-start a new release run.
P000.title: Disable automatic Release Controller branch polling

done: Backed up the live API-host Release Controller config and changed `polling_enabled=false`.
Restarted Release Controller, then deployed immutable image `127.0.0.1:5000/novaic/release-controller@sha256:71638fd91a55890ad708e47cdfb5bdee96da8a110bed2164210d9e077190a604`.
Made `ControllerConfig` reject `polling_enabled=true` and made `/v1/polls/once` diagnostic dry-run only.
Changed branch poll dry-run so it no longer advances `branch-heads.json`.
Updated architecture docs, deployment runbook, and CI guards to make explicit `/v1/triggers` the normal staging entrypoint.
Backed up and reset live `branch-heads.json` to `{}` because autonomous polling is no longer supported.
known_gaps: The branch rule mode value is still named `auto_deploy` internally; it now means a branch rule deploy action after an explicit trigger, not autonomous branch polling. This naming can be cleaned in a later schema migration if desired.
R000.summary: Implemented and deployed the manual-trigger CI/CD cutover for Release Controller.
verification: After push to `main`, live `/v1/status` stayed `polling.enabled=false`, `polling.running=false`; no automatic run was created for commit `53a901b5f0c64586b0fe9b39bc086c24afac4371`.
Live `/v1/polls/once` with `dry_run=true` plans a diagnostic run but leaves `branch_heads={}`.
Live compose status shows the Release Controller container healthy on the new immutable image digest.

acceptance_criteria: Live `/v1/status` reports polling disabled and not running.
Repository docs state staging backend/factory CI/CD starts from explicit `/v1/triggers`, not branch polling.
CI guard tests cover the manual-trigger release contract.
T000.classification_reason: Bounded operational/docs/guard change with direct live status verification and focused tests.
T000.problem_definition: The Release Controller is the centralized backend/factory CI/CD service, but the live service still has autonomous branch polling enabled. In an agent-driven workflow, a push should not implicitly start staging deployment; the release action should be explicit and auditable through the controller API.
T000.proposed_solution: Patch the live API-host Release Controller config to disable autonomous polling, restart the service, update architecture/runbook docs to make `/v1/triggers` the normal staging entrypoint, and add repository guards that reject docs/config drifting back to autonomous polling as the normal backend/factory path.
T000.title: Make Release Controller CI/CD explicitly triggered
T000.verification_plan: Use the live status API before and after restart, run focused pytest guards/unit tests, commit and push the doc/guard changes, then verify that the push does not create a new automatic release run.
